title: Distributed Governance Research: A Functional Architecture for Selective Institutional Distribution (Corpus + Code, v1.13)
description: Complete research corpus and code accompanying a working paper that proposes a functional architecture for distributed public project governance: citizens allocate a mandate-recorded share of an existing public budget across verifiable projects under strict role separation, milestone-gated conditional disbursement, evidential contracts, and role-based reputation — distributed allocation within mandate-bound, visible, versioned, and contestable planning scopes. Includes formal mechanism-design propositions (incentive compatibility of milestone disbursement; collusion-proofness of protocolized fiscalization), a reproducible 10,000-agent simulation of eight experiments (six pre-registered), plus a later pre-registered symmetric selection-only stress test (NO-GO) that retires the earlier compound value-per-budget multiplier as a calibrated effect (the distributed selection advantage is real but small; the controlling specification is research/claim-and-estimand-contract.md), and a documented adversarial review record of forty-three attack briefs across five rounds with paired defenses and accepted resolutions, all propagated through the architecture corpus. This record is the corpus + code ONLY; the working-paper manuscript (drafts/paper.md, drafts/paper.es.md) and its accessible companion (docs/101) are a SEPARATE CC BY-NC-ND record (metadata: drafts/paper.zenodo.json).
notes: This is the CORPUS/CODE deposit record (CC BY 4.0 + MIT). From v1.13 onward the MANUSCRIPT and reviewer packets are licensed CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 and are deposited as a SEPARATE Zenodo record (metadata: drafts/paper.zenodo.json) so the two licenses are not conflated in a single CC-BY record. Dual-license map: research corpus (docs/knowledge/attacks/defenses/research) CC BY 4.0; manuscript (drafts/paper.md, drafts/paper.es.md, docs/101, drafts/ensayo-divulgacion.md, drafts/explicacion-para-todos.md, external-review/) CC BY-NC-ND 4.0; code (scripts/) MIT. See LICENSE.md. (Historical note: the already-public v1.12 record bundled all content under CC BY 4.0 — an irrevocable public grant; the separation begins with v1.13.)
